anti_patterns: 'Tested and it works' without describing what was tested
'Researched the market' without a list of sources
'Fixed the bug' without explaining root cause
how_to_apply: Dev: output of build, test, lint, type check pasted (not 'I ran it and it passed'). Screenshot for UI. Grep of actual callsite to prove the right code path was touched
Design: screenshot or link to final artboard. Cross-reference to design system tokens respected
Marketing: full draft pasted. Not 'I wrote it and it's good'
Research: sources cited with URL, author, date. Raw data or screenshot of source
Writing: final text pasted. Specific excerpt when edit was point-scoped
Product (PM): link to PRD/RDD. Traceable decisions
Discipline-specific self-QA criteria are part of each Specialist's task context — this rule requires evidence exists, Leo defines what counts as good evidence per domain
content.responsibility: All agents. Leo rejects deliveries from Specialists that don't comply. Specialists must self-verify before reporting to Leo. Two-layer cascade of rigor.
content.why: Claude has a strong tendency to say 'done, passed, works' without verifying. It's structural optimism from training. 'Said it passed' and 'passed' are different things, and the user has been burned trusting the first.
content.constraint: The user shouldn't need to believe you. They should be able to check. Every delivery must come with verifiable evidence. If you don't have evidence, the work is not done.

summary: The user shouldn't need to believe you. They should be able to check. Every delivery must come with verifiable evidence
